title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Runtime Lens: Atomic Output
summary: The script reads arguments during import, mixes parsing with writes, partially replaces the public directory on failure, and cannot explain which configuration won. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the atomic output increment in import-safe deterministic build pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.
Keep deterministic transformations pure and pass filesystem, clock, environment, logging, and publication effects through narrow adapters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_cli_pipeline_2(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"

Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Atomic Output model · Theory Atomic Output, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Atomic Output precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Atomic Output model · Theory Atomic Output, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7025 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Atomic Output model · Theory Atomic Output, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Deleting output before building is the cleanest safe publication strategy.
